sections.paragraph:
ζυγός, οῦ, ὁ [parallel form of the nt. ζυγόν ‘yoke, balance’]

—1. in varying imagery of a device used to hook up two draft animals, yoke Mt 11:29f; in Ac 15:10; Gal 5:1; 1 Ti 6:1 the focus is on the oppressive nature of the one imposing the yoke.

—2. of a suspended beam used to determine weight, weighing device, pair of scales Rv 6:5.
